summary:
The characteristic feature distinguishing Tricuspid Atresia includes an abnormal QRS complex orientation towards the left side coupled with possible indicators of altered cardiac chamber pressures; thus confirming D remains correct based on consistent medical literature references provided through recent searches.
```
``` ```plaintext
The characteristic ECG finding in Tricuspid Atresia shows a leftward QRS axis due to specific electrical conduction abnormalities, differing markedly from typical patterns seen in other conditions like right-axis-deviation syndromes. Splitting of the second heart sound (S2), however, does not typically occur in cases of Tricuspid Atresia according to available data supporting option D being accurate among given choices.
```
